name: infrastructure-architecture-expert
description: Azure infrastructure architecture expert with comprehensive knowledge of cloud design patterns, architecture styles, Well-Architected Framework, cloud technologies, and Azure database services (SQL Server and PostgreSQL)

training_content: Azure Architecture, Microservices, Data Architecture, Enterprise Architecture, Security Tools
Azure Database Services Documentation - 4 comprehensive guides (~69,000 words)
sources.version_info: Azure services as of October 2024, Pluralsight content 2024, Azure Database Services October 2025

can_do: Explain Azure cloud design patterns (37 patterns)
Recommend architecture styles based on requirements
Guide Azure service selection (compute, data, networking)
Compare data mesh vs data fabric architectures
Provide high availability and disaster recovery patterns
Compare Azure database deployment models (SQL Database, SQL MI, PostgreSQL Flexible, Hyperscale)
Recommend database selection based on requirements (SQL Server vs PostgreSQL)
Explain database migration strategies and tools (DMS, SSMA)
Provide database cost optimization guidance (AHUB, Reserved Capacity)
Describe database HA and DR configurations
Guide Azure database service integrations (Synapse, Power BI, Data Factory)
cannot_do: Make specific architectural decisions without context (opinion-based)
Choose between equally valid approaches without requirements
Make cost-benefit trade-off decisions without business context
Choose technology stack (requires business and team context)

pattern_catalog.statement: Azure Architecture Center documents 37 cloud design patterns organized into 8 categories: Design & Implementation, Resiliency, Data Management, Messaging, Performance & Scalability, Security, Management & Monitoring, and Availability
pattern_catalog.source: Azure Architecture Center - Cloud Design Patterns

circuit_breaker.statement: Circuit Breaker pattern prevents an application from repeatedly trying to execute an operation that's likely to fail. It has three states: Closed (normal operation), Open (fast-fail), and Half-Open (testing recovery)
circuit_breaker.source: Azure Architecture Center - Circuit Breaker Pattern
circuit_breaker.use_case: Prevents cascade failures, provides fast failure

retry_pattern.statement: Retry pattern handles transient failures by transparently retrying failed operations with strategies like exponential backoff, fixed interval, or incremental intervals. Operations must be idempotent for safe retries
retry_pattern.requirement: Idempotency is mandatory for safe retries

cqrs.statement: CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) separates read and update operations for a data store, using different models optimized for each. Enables independent scaling and optimized query performance but introduces eventual consistency
when_to_use: Read and write workloads differ significantly

eight_styles.statement: There are 8 fundamental architecture styles for cloud applications: N-tier, Web-Queue-Worker, Microservices, Event-Driven, Big Data, CQRS, Serverless, and Space-Based. Each has specific characteristics, benefits, challenges, and appropriate use cases

microservices.statement: Microservices architecture composes applications from small, independent services that communicate through well-defined APIs. Benefits include independent deployment, independent scaling, technology diversity, fault isolation, and team autonomy. Challenges include distributed system complexity, eventual consistency, and requiring DevOps maturity
microservices.source: Azure Architecture Center + Pluralsight Microservices Training

event_driven.statement: Event-Driven Architecture uses events to communicate between decoupled services. Producers don't know consumers, and consumers decide which events to handle. Three models: Event Streaming (continuous flow), Simple Event Processing (immediate action), Complex Event Processing (pattern analysis)

five_pillars.statement: Azure Well-Architected Framework consists of five pillars: Reliability (system recovery and continuity), Security (defense-in-depth and identity), Cost Optimization (resource efficiency and waste elimination), Operational Excellence (deployment practices and monitoring), and Performance Efficiency (scaling to meet demands)

reliability.statement: Reliability is the ability of a system to recover from failures and continue to function. Key metrics: MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time To Recovery), RTO (Recovery Time Objective), RPO (Recovery Point Objective), SLA (Service Level Agreement)

security.statement: Security in Azure follows defense-in-depth strategy with 7 layers: Physical security (Azure's responsibility), Identity and access, Perimeter, Network, Compute, Application, and Data. Identity is the primary security perimeter in cloud
principles: Encryption at rest and in transit

cost_optimization.statement: Cost optimization strategies include right-sizing resources, using Reserved Instances (up to 72% savings), Azure Hybrid Benefit (up to 40% for VMs, up to 55% for SQL), Spot VMs (up to 90% savings), auto-scaling, serverless, and storage tiering
cost_optimization.source: Azure Well-Architected Framework - Cost Optimization + Pluralsight Training
anti_patterns: Using Reserved Instances for 90%+ of capacity

compute_options.statement: Azure offers 10+ compute services spanning IaaS to serverless: Virtual Machines (full control), AKS (Kubernetes), Container Apps (serverless containers), Container Instances, App Service (PaaS web apps), Functions (serverless FaaS), Static Web Apps, Batch (HPC), Logic Apps (workflow automation)

availability_zones.statement: Availability Zones are physically separate locations within an Azure region, each with independent power, cooling, and networking. They provide 99.99% SLA for VMs deployed across zones (vs 99.9% for single-zone). Minimum 3 zones per supported region
availability_zones.source: Azure Availability Zones Documentation + Pluralsight HA Training
availability_zones.vs_availability_sets: Zones = datacenter-level isolation, Sets = rack-level isolation within single datacenter

vm_scale_sets.statement: VM Scale Sets create and manage a group of load-balanced VMs with auto-scaling based on CPU, memory, or custom metrics. Supports up to 1000 VMs with platform images, 600 with custom images. Provides automatic OS upgrades and load balancing integration
vm_scale_sets.source: Azure VM Scale Sets Documentation + Pluralsight Training

site_recovery.statement: Azure Site Recovery (ASR) provides disaster recovery for VMs and physical servers, replicating to Azure or secondary site. Supports Azure-to-Azure, VMware-to-Azure, Hyper-V-to-Azure, and Physical-to-Azure. RPO typically 30 seconds to 5 minutes
site_recovery.source: Azure Site Recovery Documentation + Pluralsight DR Training
site_recovery.vs_backup: ASR = disaster recovery (RTO minutes), Backup = data protection (RTO hours)

definition.statement: Azure landing zones are pre-configured environments with governance, security, and networking infrastructure. Seven design areas: Management, Governance, Security, Network topology, Identity, Platform automation, Resource organization

seven_rs.statement: The 7 Rs of cloud migration: Rehost (lift-and-shift), Replatform (lift-tinker-shift), Refactor (re-architect), Rebuild (redesign from scratch), Replace (drop and shop SaaS), Retire (decommission), Retain (revisit later)
seven_rs.tool: Azure Migrate for assessment and migration

communication.statement: Microservices communication patterns: RPI (Request/Response) for synchronous calls, Messaging (event-based) for asynchronous. Use API Gateway pattern for external clients, Service Registry for discovery, Circuit Breaker for fault tolerance

bounded_contexts.statement: Bounded contexts from Domain-Driven Design define clear boundaries for microservices. Each service owns its domain model and data. Services are small, autonomous, and built around business capabilities (2-pizza team rule)

data_mesh.statement: Data mesh is decentralized data architecture with four core principles: (1) Domain-oriented data ownership - each business domain owns its data, (2) Data as a product - teams treat datasets with product thinking, (3) Self-serve data infrastructure - platform for domains to publish/consume independently, (4) Federated computational governance - policies defined collectively but enforced locally
when_to_use: Large organizations with multiple business domains

data_fabric.statement: Data fabric is unified architecture with metadata-driven automation for seamless data access across heterogeneous systems. Features: automated data integration, centralized governance, real-time and batch processing, unified access layer
data_fabric.vs_mesh: Fabric = centralized with unified access, Mesh = decentralized with domain ownership

togaf.statement: TOGAF (The Open Group Architecture Framework) provides methodology for enterprise architecture with Architecture Development Method (ADM) - 8-phase iterative cycle: Preliminary, Architecture Vision, Business Architecture, Information Systems Architecture, Technology Architecture, Opportunities & Solutions, Migration Planning, Implementation Governance, Architecture Change Management

zachman.statement: Zachman Framework is classification schema for organizing enterprise architecture artifacts using 6x6 matrix: rows (Planner, Owner, Designer, Builder, Implementer, Worker) × columns (What, How, Where, Who, When, Why)
zachman.purpose: Classification and organization of architecture artifacts, not a methodology

zero_trust.statement: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture has 7 tenets: (1) All data sources and computing services are resources, (2) All communication secured regardless of location, (3) Access granted per-session, (4) Access determined by dynamic policy, (5) Monitor and measure integrity and posture, (6) Authentication and authorization are dynamic and strictly enforced, (7) Collect info about asset/network/communications

soc2.statement: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria has 5 categories: Security (common criteria for all), Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy. Based on COSO framework with 17 common criteria for security

terrascan.statement: Terrascan is open-source static code analyzer for IaC scanning Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes manifests, and Dockerfiles. Implements NIST Cybersecurity Framework. Can run as pre-commit hook, standalone server, GitHub action, or CLI. Covers 500+ rules across AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes
terrascan.recommendation: Use as pre-commit hook to block high/critical violations before commit

renewable_regions.statement: Azure sustainability practices include renewable energy regions, blob storage lifecycle management (hot/cool/cold/archive tiers), right-sizing to eliminate waste, using Azure Sustainability Calculator for carbon footprint tracking

sql_database_paas.statement: Azure SQL Database is fully managed PaaS with 99.99% SLA. Deployment: Single Database or Elastic Pools. Purchasing: DTU (bundled) or vCore (flexible). Tiers: Basic/Standard/Premium (DTU), General Purpose/Business Critical/Hyperscale (vCore). Max size: 100TB (Hyperscale). Features: automatic backups (7-35 days), point-in-time restore, geo-replication, read scale-out, serverless tier

sql_managed_instance.statement: Azure SQL Managed Instance is PaaS with near 100% SQL Server compatibility. Features: SQL Server Agent, cross-database queries, CLR assemblies, Service Broker, linked servers. Requires VNet integration. Tiers: General Purpose (99.99% SLA, 4-80 vCores, 32GB-16TB) and Business Critical (99.99-99.995% SLA, 4-80 vCores, 32GB-4TB, local SSD, in-memory OLTP)
sql_managed_instance.source: Microsoft Azure SQL Managed Instance Documentation

sql_server_vm.statement: SQL Server on Azure VMs is IaaS with full SQL Server compatibility. Full control over SQL Server installation, any version (2008-2022), any edition. Storage: Premium SSD or Ultra Disk. HA options: Always On Availability Groups, Failover Cluster Instances, Azure Site Recovery. SQL IaaS Extension: automated backup, automated patching, Azure Key Vault integration
sql_server_vm.source: Microsoft SQL Server in Azure VMs Documentation
use_cases: Applications requiring full SQL Server features
licensing: Azure Hybrid Benefit (BYOL, save up to 55%)

postgresql_flexible.statement: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server is recommended PaaS for PostgreSQL (versions 11-16). Tiers: Burstable (1-2 vCores, dev/test), General Purpose (2-64 vCores, balanced), Memory Optimized (2-64 vCores, high-performance). Storage: 32GB-32TB Premium SSD, auto-grow, max 20,000 IOPS. HA: zone-redundant (99.99% SLA, < 120s RPO, < 60s RTO). Features: Built-in PgBouncer, read replicas (up to 5), 50+ extensions (PostGIS, TimescaleDB, pg_cron)
postgresql_flexible.source: Microsoft Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server Documentation

postgresql_hyperscale.statement: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Hyperscale (Citus) is distributed PostgreSQL for massive scale using sharding. Architecture: 1 coordinator node + 2-20 worker nodes. Sharding: hash (default) or append distribution. Features: linear scalability, parallel query execution, distributed JOINs, 10-100x faster for sharded workloads. Max size: petabytes (distributed). 99.99% SLA with shard replication
use_cases: Multi-tenant SaaS (100+ to millions of tenants)

database_selection.statement: Database selection criteria: (1) Technology - SQL Server (existing apps, Microsoft ecosystem, AHUB), PostgreSQL (open-source, advanced features, lower cost), MySQL (LAMP stack, simplicity); (2) Deployment - PaaS (minimal management) vs IaaS (full control); (3) Scale - Single-node (< 100GB) vs Hyperscale (> 100GB, massive scale); (4) Cost - PostgreSQL 30-50% lower than SQL Server without AHUB
decision_factors: Licensing costs (SQL Server commercial, PostgreSQL free)
database_selection.paas_vs_iaas: PaaS = automatic patching/backup/HA, limited features; IaaS = full control, self-managed, 100% compatibility

cost_optimization.statement: Azure database cost optimization: (1) Azure Hybrid Benefit - up to 55% savings with SQL Server licenses+SA for SQL Database, SQL MI, SQL VM; (2) Reserved Capacity - 1-3 year commitment, save up to 80% for SQL and 65% for PostgreSQL; (3) Elastic Pools - share resources among multiple SQL databases; (4) Serverless tier - auto-pause, pay only for compute used; (5) Right-sizing based on metrics; (6) Burstable tier PostgreSQL - stop/start capability for dev/test
cost_optimization.ahub_eligibility: SQL Server Enterprise or Standard with Software Assurance
cost_optimization.cost_comparison: 8 vCores, 512GB, HA: PostgreSQL $1,200/month vs SQL Database $2,400/month (without AHUB) or $1,800/month (with AHUB)

migration_tools.statement: Azure Database Migration Service (DMS) supports online (minimal downtime) and offline migrations. Scenarios: SQL Server → SQL Database/MI/VM, PostgreSQL → Flexible Server/Hyperscale, MySQL → Flexible Server, Oracle → PostgreSQL/SQL (via SSMA), AWS RDS → Azure. Features: assessment reports, schema migration, data migration, validation, cutover coordination. SQL Server Migration Assistant (SSMA) converts Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SAP ASE, Access to SQL Server/Azure SQL
migration_tools.source: Microsoft Azure Database Migration Service Documentation
migration_tools.online_migration: Continuous data sync with minimal downtime (minutes), best for production
migration_tools.offline_migration: Downtime during migration, faster, best for non-production or maintenance windows

service_integrations.statement: Azure database integrations: (1) Analytics - Synapse Analytics (data warehousing), Power BI (Direct Query or Import); (2) ETL - Data Factory (100+ connectors, orchestration), Event Hubs (event streaming); (3) Application - Functions (serverless APIs, SQL bindings), Logic Apps (workflow automation); (4) Monitoring - Azure Monitor (metrics/alerts), Application Insights (APM), Log Analytics; (5) Security - Azure AD (authentication), Key Vault (secrets), Private Link (network security)
service_integrations.synapse_integration: PolyBase for querying SQL Database from Synapse, Copy Activity for ETL
service_integrations.power_bi_modes: Direct Query (real-time, live connection) or Import (faster rendering, scheduled refresh)
service_integrations.managed_identity: Password-less authentication using Azure managed identities for apps to databases

high_availability.statement: Azure database HA: (1) SQL Database - 99.99% SLA (Premium/BC), automatic backups every 5-10min, geo-replication (up to 4 readable secondaries), auto-failover groups; (2) SQL MI - 99.99-99.995% SLA, Always On built-in, zone-redundant (BC tier), auto-failover groups for DR; (3) PostgreSQL Flexible - 99.9% SLA (same-zone) or 99.99% SLA (zone-redundant), synchronous replication, automatic failover < 120s RPO, < 60s RTO; (4) PostgreSQL Hyperscale - 99.99% SLA, shard replication (1-2 replicas per shard)
high_availability.rpo_rto: RPO = Recovery Point Objective (data loss tolerance), RTO = Recovery Time Objective (downtime tolerance)
high_availability.zone_redundancy: Distributes replicas across availability zones for datacenter-level resilience

security_compliance.statement: Azure database security: All services support TLS 1.2/1.3 encryption (in-transit), AES-256 encryption (at-rest), Azure AD authentication, Private Link/VNet integration, customer-managed keys (Key Vault), row-level security, auditing, threat detection. Compliance: HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, ISO 27018, GDPR, IRS 1075, FedRAMP (SQL services). SQL Server specific: Transparent Data Encryption (TDE), Always Encrypted, Dynamic Data Masking
security_compliance.source: Microsoft Azure Security and Compliance Documentation
security_compliance.private_link: Access database over private IP in VNet, no public internet exposure, required for many compliance scenarios

notes.maintenance: Update when Azure releases new services, patterns, Well-Architected Framework updates, or database service updates
notes.coverage: Covers 37 cloud design patterns, 8 architecture styles, 5 Well-Architected pillars, microservices patterns, data architecture (mesh/fabric), enterprise architecture (TOGAF/Zachman), compliance (Zero Trust, SOC 2, IRS 1075), IaC security, and Azure database services (SQL Server and PostgreSQL deployment models, selection frameworks, migrations, integrations)
notes.version_history: v2.0.0 - Complete rebuild to match expert design specification (2025-10-17); v2.1.0 - Added Azure database services documentation (2025-10-17)
